title: verify-reproduction output
description:
Result of a reproduction comparison.

A mismatch or incomparable is a SUCCESSFUL run (exit-0, needs_decision=True)
— a discovered nondeterminism is the feature working, never an error.

stage_reached.description: Overall verdict. v1: match / mismatch (any key mismatched) / incomparable. v2 also emits auto_cleared (every deviation inside a well-evidenced envelope — a code attestation, zero human attention) and needs_verdict (a thin-envelope / novelty / incomparable residue routed to the human with the evidence brief).
stage_reached.title: Stage Reached

needs_decision.description: True for a FINDING the human decides on (mismatch / incomparable / needs_verdict); False when the comparison auto-cleared (exact match or well-evidenced envelope).
needs_decision.title: Needs Decision

reason.description: Code-rendered one-line summary: matched/mismatched/incomparable key counts + verdict.
reason.title: Reason

receipt.description: The full receipt record appended to reproduction_receipts.jsonl (self-contained).
receipt.title: Receipt

receipt_path.description: Absolute path of the append-only receipts ledger this verification appended to.
receipt_path.title: Receipt Path

appended_sample.description: The determinism-fingerprint sample this comparison appended to the experiment's ledger (D-consume: verify appends the comparison as a new sample). Null when no sample was minted (e.g. a v1-only comparison, or missing artifacts). Echoed so a consumer sees the evidence just recorded.

diverged_stage.description: Stage-localized mismatch (the data-trace fingerprint interlock): the first diverging stage by pipeline order when both runs carry ingested traces and the verdict routed to the human — the brief renders 'diverges at <stage>' from this machine field. Null otherwise.
diverged_stage.title: Diverged Stage

DeterminismSampleRecord.description:
One append-only ledger line — a single observed run-to-run comparison.

A valid ``state/attestation.py::validate`` record: the attestation fields
(``attestor``, ``subject_kind``, ``subject_id``, ``content_sha``) make each
line bind-lockable, so a spread cannot be asserted into existence. The
subject is the experiment IDENTITY, not one run — original and reproduction
samples accumulate to the SAME ledger, keyed on ``subject_id`` (cmd_sha).

schema_version.description: Sample-record schema version (bump on shape change).
schema_version.title: Schema Version

attestor.description: Always code — a sample is a code attestation.
attestor.title: Attestor

subject_id.description: The experiment identity this sample attests (cmd_sha).
subject_id.title: Subject Id

content_sha.description: Canonical SHA over the two COMPARED on-disk payloads (harness-contract canonicalization) — the bind recompute key and the admission join key.
content_sha.title: Content Sha

cluster.description: The measuring cluster (cross-cluster spread is an env-sensitivity finding).
cluster.title: Cluster

verdict.description: The comparison's verdict AT APPEND (judgment precedes append).
verdict.title: Verdict

same_submission.description: True for a double-canary pair (one environment observed twice) — a correlated-sample label.
same_submission.title: Same Submission

partial.description: True for a partial-reproduction sample (subset of tasks).
partial.title: Partial

task_indices.description: The exact task indices compared on a partial sample; null for a full sample.
task_indices.title: Task Indices

per_key.description: Per-flattened-key observed pairs for this comparison.
per_key.title: Per Key

DeterminismSampleRecord.title: determinism fingerprint sample record

SampleIdentity.description:
The experiment IDENTITY every sample carries verbatim (the ``_IDENTITY_FIELDS``
discipline) — the CURRENT-identity filter compares against these, reading a
``tasks_py_sha``-drifted prior STALE.

data_sha.description: Data-identity leg (Phase-3 amendment, ruled 0b): the data-manifest sha of the declared input roots at record time, lifted from the run sidecar's data_manifest_sha. ADDITIVE + optional — null is 'data identity unknown (no manifest at record time)', disclosed and never blocking. A sample under DIFFERENT data is excluded as data drift, never admitted as nondeterminism evidence; a pre-amendment record (no data_sha) parses byte-identically.
data_sha.title: Data Sha

SampleIdentity.title: determinism sample identity

SampleKeyDiff.description:
One flattened metric leaf's observed pair in a sample (D-store ``per_key``).

``a`` / ``b`` are the two compared VALUES — opaque scalars, never named. The
diff fields are null for non-numeric leaves (equality-only), present for
float leaves.

key.description: Flattened metric key (opaque path; the comparator never reads its meaning).
key.title: Key

a.description: Value observed on side A (opaque scalar).
a.title: A

b.description: Value observed on side B (opaque scalar).
b.title: B

abs_diff.description: |a - b| for numeric leaves; null otherwise.
abs_diff.title: Abs Diff

rel_diff.description: |a - b| / max(|a|, |b|) for numeric leaves; null otherwise.
rel_diff.title: Rel Diff

static_class.description: Structural class of this leaf (only float is tolerance-eligible).
static_class.title: Static Class

SampleKeyDiff.title: determinism sample per-key diff
